Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM for PivotPoint deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "PivotPoint" (or similar) or by looking up the PivotPoint company first via crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint" or similar) to get company_id "company_pivot" and then searching deals by company_id. Expected findings: Agent discovers deal_pivot_april_2026_dual in "inprogress" stage, amount $10,650, close date 2026-05-09, description detailing the Okonjo + Castañeda dual relocation with H-1B visa coordination and CNC equipment freight, invoice INV-2026-0315 issued. Also discovers deal_pivot_q2 in "closedwon" stage ($5,803, Ines Kovac relocation).
OE 2: Look up CRM engagements for PivotPoint using crm_list_engagements with company_ids ["company_pivot"] (or similar), or by listing engagements without filters to discover all related notes. Expected findings: Agent discovers engagement_pivot_april_2026_dual_reloc (linked to company_pivot) containing a detailed account note with email thread summary, coordinator assignments (Fatimah Al-Rashidi for Nadia, Javier Morales for Diego), vendor references (the engagement's coordinator assignments section lists Swift Relocations for Nadia and Heartland Movers for Diego, though the email thread summary mentions Swift Relocations in the context of Diego's CNC crating), key complications (H-1B visa timing with attorney Lorraine Pham, CNC mill freight requirements, 16-day storage gap, pre-move crating inspection), and financial breakdown (total $10,650 including surcharges totaling $3,610). May also discover engagement_pivot_apr09_account_update_invoice (which has no company_id linked, so may require broader search) noting that the invoice was issued while coordinator execution was newly handed off. Agent may additionally encounter engagement_pivot_apr08_additional_austin_moves (linked to company_pivot) referencing additional Austin relocation requests from Sam Delgado for Priya Raman and Elliot Mercer, which is noise/context from scenario generation and not directly relevant to the core PivotPoint gaps.
OE 3: Search emails for Sam Delgado's escalation using search_emails with query "Sam Delgado" or "PivotPoint" or "INV-2026-0315" (or similar). Expected findings: Agent discovers email_sam_delgado_direct_escalation_apr25 from sam@pivotpointlabs.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com), subject "Urgent: PivotPoint relocations, invoice INV-2026-0315, and account risk," dated April 25, 2026. The email details four critical issues: (1) Nadia Okonjo's move missed the April 14-18 window and no one from MoveOps contacted immigration attorney Lorraine Pham regarding the USCIS address-change requirement; (2) Diego Castañeda's crating, pickup, storage, and delivery chain was never verified and no vendor was confirmed as booked; (3) invoice INV-2026-0315 was issued for work that appears unexecuted; (4) Sam was unable to give an unqualified positive recommendation about MoveOps to "a biotech operator" who asked whether MoveOps could handle sensitive employee relocations at scale. Agent may also discover the follow-up email email_david_unanswered_pivotpoint_escalation_apr26 from Sam on April 26 restating urgency.
OE 4: Search Airtable for Nadia Okonjo's relocation record using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Nadia Okonjo" (or similar), or by listing records from the Relocations table. Expected findings: Agent finds record recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin with Status "In Progress," Company "PivotPoint Labs," Origin "San Francisco, CA," Destination "Austin, TX," Move Start Date "2026-04-14," Move End Date "2026-04-18," Assigned Coordinator "Fatimah Al-Rashidi." The move window (April 14-18) has passed as of the universe date (April 26) with no status update to "Completed," confirming the relocation was not completed on schedule. Special Requirements note H-1B visa dependency requiring coordination with immigration attorney Lorraine Pham (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) before scheduling, 5th-floor Victorian walkup with $500 surcharge, and Account Manager Catalina Dubois.
OE 5: Search Airtable for Diego Castañeda's relocation record using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Diego" or "Castañeda" (or similar). Expected findings: Agent finds record recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin with Status "In Progress," Company "PivotPoint Labs," Origin "Brooklyn, NY," Destination "Austin, TX," Move Start Date "2026-04-12," Move End Date "2026-05-02," Assigned Coordinator "Javier Morales." Special Requirements detail a Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill (~600 lbs, insured value $42,000), pre-move crating inspection requested for April 10-11 (now past), 16-day storage gap (Brooklyn lease ended April 15, Austin apartment available May 1), climate-controlled storage requirement with loading dock access, and cargo insurance minimum $50K. The pre-move inspection window and Brooklyn lease end date have both passed, indicating critical logistics steps may have been missed. Account Manager: Catalina Dubois.
OE 6: Search CRM for active prospect and early-pipeline deals using crm_search_deals with broad queries (or similar) to identify deals in prospecting, appointmentscheduled, or qualifiedtobuy stages. Expected findings: Agent discovers several prospect-stage deals including (a) deal_foxglove_qualified_apr24 for Foxglove Biotech at "qualifiedtobuy" stage, $5,800, close date 2026-05-15; (b) deal_harbour_001 for Harbour Pharmaceutical at "qualifiedtobuy" stage, $4,800, close date 2026-04-15 (already passed, indicating a stalled deal); (c) deal_driftwood_001 for Driftwood Analytics at "appointmentscheduled" stage, $15,000, close date 2026-05-31; (d) crm_deal_a95b4721982a for Ridgeline Robotics at "Qualified To Buy" stage, $8,750, close date 2026-05-08. Agent should note that Harbour's close date has passed and Driftwood has been at appointmentscheduled since mid-March, both indicating deals that have stalled or gone quiet.
OE 7: Cross-reference prospect deals with emails using search_emails with queries related to "Foxglove" or "Harbour" or "Driftwood" or "Ridgeline" (or similar). Expected findings: (a) Foxglove: Agent discovers email_foxglove_nadia_due_diligence_apr24 from n.ellsworth@foxglovebiotech.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (April 24, 2026) raising concerns about "a small product design firm in Austin" with four specific issues: missed move windows, an employee left waiting for a relocation that never happened, weeks of unanswered emails to the account manager, and invoicing issued before services were delivered. This description matches PivotPoint Labs exactly, connecting the PivotPoint escalation to a prospect deal at risk. Also discovers email_mina_foxglove_due_diligence_unanswered_apr26 (April 26) from Nadia Ellsworth following up because Mina has not yet responded. (b) Harbour: Agent finds Catalina's initial outreach to c.fontaine@harbourpharma.com in late January, d.kowalski@harbourpharma.com's reply with a detailed relocation inquiry for a senior computational biologist, and Catalina's substantive proposal response, but no further activity or deal progression after the proposal, confirming the deal has stalled past its April 15 close date. (c) Ridgeline: Agent finds email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1 from kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com (April 21) following up on Anya Petrova's warm introduction, and David's belated reply email_email_57faf42664a0 (April 24). (d) Driftwood: Agent finds Mina's outreach to r.huang@driftwoodanalytics.com, a reply from k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com with detailed questions about timeline and employee count, and Mina's substantive follow-up response, but no recent activity since that exchange, indicating the deal has gone quiet at appointmentscheduled.
OE 8: Search Slack for prospect and pipeline signals using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Ridgeline" or "prospect" or "pipeline" (or similar). Expected findings: Agent discovers David's message in #customer-engagement (C002) from April 25 acknowledging that the Ridgeline follow-up was delayed ("I just checked the actual trail on Ridgeline and this is on me... Kenji's 4/21 follow-up is still sitting unanswered in my inbox"), confirming awareness of the dropped ball. May also find messages about prospect pipeline momentum and StormCloud referral context.
OE 9: Create a CRM engagement to log the PivotPoint account gaps using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", body containing a summary of investigation findings: Nadia Okonjo's relocation missed the April 14-18 move window with no evidence immigration attorney Lorraine Pham was contacted, Diego Castañeda's pre-move crating inspection and storage/delivery chain appear unverified past the April 10-11 window, invoice INV-2026-0315 ($10,650) was issued for work that may not have been fully executed, and Sam Delgado escalated directly to David Chen bypassing Catalina Dubois. Company_ids should include "company_pivot" (or similar).
OE 10: Create a Linear issue for ops follow-up on the PivotPoint relocations using linear_create_issue with team referencing an operations team (discovered via linear_list_teams, or created via linear_create_team if none exist), title related to PivotPoint relocation gaps or recovery plan (or similar), and description detailing: (1) Nadia Okonjo SF-to-Austin relocation missed April 14-18 window, immigration attorney Lorraine Pham may not have been contacted, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi assigned but no follow-through visible; (2) Diego Castañeda Brooklyn-to-Austin equipment relocation has unverified crating/storage/delivery chain, coordinator Javier Morales assigned, pre-move inspection window passed; (3) invoice INV-2026-0315 for $10,650 issued but service execution unclear; (4) client CEO Sam Delgado has escalated directly.
OE 11: Send Sam Delgado a check-in email using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["sam@pivotpointlabs.com"], subject related to PivotPoint account status or check-in, or similar) or reply_to_email (email_id: "email_sam_delgado_direct_escalation_apr25" or "email_david_unanswered_pivotpoint_escalation_apr26", sender: "david.chen@moveops.com"), with content acknowledging Sam's escalation, summarizing the current status of both Nadia Okonjo's and Diego Castañeda's relocations based on investigation findings, addressing the invoice INV-2026-0315 concern, and outlining next steps or a recovery plan for the account.
OE 12: Send follow-up emails to stalled or at-risk prospects using send_email or reply_to_email. The agent should identify prospects that need attention based on investigation findings and send appropriate follow-up emails. Key candidates: (a) Harbour Pharmaceutical (close date April 15 has passed, deal stalled) -- email to c.fontaine@harbourpharma.com (Claire Fontaine, the actual correspondent -- note: the most recent Harbour email was sent from d.kowalski@harbourpharma.com but is signed by Claire Fontaine and Catalina's reply addresses "Claire," indicating a shared mailbox or alias) or d.kowalski@harbourpharma.com (the sender address on the most recent email); (b) Foxglove Biotech (due diligence concern about MoveOps from Nadia Ellsworth is unanswered by Mina) -- the agent may send a follow-up or flag for Mina; (c) Driftwood Analytics (deal at appointmentscheduled since March with no recent activity). Sender should be david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 13: Update CRM deal stages and engagement notes using crm_update_deal_stage and/or crm_create_engagement to reflect investigation findings across both the PivotPoint and prospect tracks. This may include updating deal stages for stalled prospects (e.g., Harbour Pharmaceutical whose close date has passed) and adding engagement notes summarizing prospect pipeline status, recent signals from cross-referencing emails and Slack, and any follow-up actions taken.
OE 14: Notify Catalina Dubois about PivotPoint account issues using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["catalina.dubois@moveops.com"]) or reply_to_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com") or conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002" for #customer-engagement, or a DM, payload: summary message, user_id: "moveops_david_chen"). Content should inform Catalina of the findings from the PivotPoint account review: Sam Delgado's direct escalation, the gaps in both Nadia and Diego relocations, the invoice concern, and any actions taken (ticket created, email sent to Sam). Should also mention any prospect pipeline issues that need her attention, particularly if Harbour Pharmaceutical (her prospect) has stalled.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM for PivotPoint deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "PivotPoint" (or similar) or by looking up the PivotPoint company first via crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint" or similar) to get company_id "company_pivot" and then searching deals by company_id. Expected findings: Agent discovers deal_pivot_april_2026_dual in "inprogress" stage, amount $10,650, close date 2026-05-09, description detailing the Okonjo + Castañeda dual relocation with H-1B visa coordination and CNC equipment freight, invoice INV-2026-0315 issued. Also discovers deal_pivot_q2 in "closedwon" stage ($5,803, Ines Kovac relocation).
OE 2: Look up CRM engagements for PivotPoint using crm_list_engagements with company_ids ["company_pivot"] (or similar), or by listing engagements without filters to discover all related notes. Expected findings: Agent discovers engagement_pivot_april_2026_dual_reloc (linked to company_pivot) containing a detailed account note with email thread summary, coordinator assignments (Fatimah Al-Rashidi for Nadia, Javier Morales for Diego), vendor references (the engagement's coordinator assignments section lists Swift Relocations for Nadia and Heartland Movers for Diego, though the email thread summary mentions Swift Relocations in the context of Diego's CNC crating), key complications (H-1B visa timing with attorney Lorraine Pham, CNC mill freight requirements, 16-day storage gap, pre-move crating inspection), and financial breakdown (total $10,650 including surcharges totaling $3,610). May also discover engagement_pivot_apr09_account_update_invoice (which has no company_id linked, so may require broader search) noting that the invoice was issued while coordinator execution was newly handed off. Agent may additionally encounter engagement_pivot_apr08_additional_austin_moves (linked to company_pivot) referencing additional Austin relocation requests from Sam Delgado for Priya Raman and Elliot Mercer, which is noise/context from scenario generation and not directly relevant to the core PivotPoint gaps.
OE 3: Search emails for Sam Delgado's escalation using search_emails with query "Sam Delgado" or "PivotPoint" or "INV-2026-0315" (or similar). Expected findings: Agent discovers email_sam_delgado_direct_escalation_apr25 from sam@pivotpointlabs.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com), subject "Urgent: PivotPoint relocations, invoice INV-2026-0315, and account risk," dated April 25, 2026. The email details four critical issues: (1) Nadia Okonjo's move missed the April 14-18 window and no one from MoveOps contacted immigration attorney Lorraine Pham regarding the USCIS address-change requirement; (2) Diego Castañeda's crating, pickup, storage, and delivery chain was never verified and no vendor was confirmed as booked; (3) invoice INV-2026-0315 was issued for work that appears unexecuted; (4) Sam was unable to give an unqualified positive recommendation about MoveOps to "a biotech operator" who asked whether MoveOps could handle sensitive employee relocations at scale. Agent may also discover the follow-up email email_david_unanswered_pivotpoint_escalation_apr26 from Sam on April 26 restating urgency.
OE 4: Search Airtable for Nadia Okonjo's relocation record using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Nadia Okonjo" (or similar), or by listing records from the Relocations table. Expected findings: Agent finds record recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin with Status "In Progress," Company "PivotPoint Labs," Origin "San Francisco, CA," Destination "Austin, TX," Move Start Date "2026-04-14," Move End Date "2026-04-18," Assigned Coordinator "Fatimah Al-Rashidi." The move window (April 14-18) has passed as of the universe date (April 26) with no status update to "Completed," confirming the relocation was not completed on schedule. Special Requirements note H-1B visa dependency requiring coordination with immigration attorney Lorraine Pham (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) before scheduling, 5th-floor Victorian walkup with $500 surcharge, and Account Manager Catalina Dubois.
OE 5: Search Airtable for Diego Castañeda's relocation record using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Diego" or "Castañeda" (or similar). Expected findings: Agent finds record recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin with Status "In Progress," Company "PivotPoint Labs," Origin "Brooklyn, NY," Destination "Austin, TX," Move Start Date "2026-04-12," Move End Date "2026-05-02," Assigned Coordinator "Javier Morales." Special Requirements detail a Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill (~600 lbs, insured value $42,000), pre-move crating inspection requested for April 10-11 (now past), 16-day storage gap (Brooklyn lease ended April 15, Austin apartment available May 1), climate-controlled storage requirement with loading dock access, and cargo insurance minimum $50K. The pre-move inspection window and Brooklyn lease end date have both passed, indicating critical logistics steps may have been missed. Account Manager: Catalina Dubois.
OE 6: Search CRM for active prospect and early-pipeline deals using crm_search_deals with broad queries (or similar) to identify deals in prospecting, appointmentscheduled, or qualifiedtobuy stages. Expected findings: Agent discovers several prospect-stage deals including (a) deal_foxglove_qualified_apr24 for Foxglove Biotech at "qualifiedtobuy" stage, $5,800, close date 2026-05-15; (b) deal_harbour_001 for Harbour Pharmaceutical at "qualifiedtobuy" stage, $4,800, close date 2026-04-15 (already passed, indicating a stalled deal); (c) deal_driftwood_001 for Driftwood Analytics at "appointmentscheduled" stage, $15,000, close date 2026-05-31; (d) crm_deal_a95b4721982a for Ridgeline Robotics at "Qualified To Buy" stage, $8,750, close date 2026-05-08. Agent should note that Harbour's close date has passed and Driftwood has been at appointmentscheduled since mid-March, both indicating deals that have stalled or gone quiet.
OE 7: Cross-reference prospect deals with emails using search_emails with queries related to "Foxglove" or "Harbour" or "Driftwood" or "Ridgeline" (or similar). Expected findings: (a) Foxglove: Agent discovers email_foxglove_nadia_due_diligence_apr24 from n.ellsworth@foxglovebiotech.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (April 24, 2026) raising concerns about "a small product design firm in Austin" with four specific issues: missed move windows, an employee left waiting for a relocation that never happened, weeks of unanswered emails to the account manager, and invoicing issued before services were delivered. This description matches PivotPoint Labs exactly, connecting the PivotPoint escalation to a prospect deal at risk. Also discovers email_mina_foxglove_due_diligence_unanswered_apr26 (April 26) from Nadia Ellsworth following up because Mina has not yet responded. (b) Harbour: Agent finds Catalina's initial outreach to c.fontaine@harbourpharma.com in late January, d.kowalski@harbourpharma.com's reply with a detailed relocation inquiry for a senior computational biologist, and Catalina's substantive proposal response, but no further activity or deal progression after the proposal, confirming the deal has stalled past its April 15 close date. (c) Ridgeline: Agent finds email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1 from kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com (April 21) following up on Anya Petrova's warm introduction, and David's belated reply email_email_57faf42664a0 (April 24). (d) Driftwood: Agent finds Mina's outreach to r.huang@driftwoodanalytics.com, a reply from k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com with detailed questions about timeline and employee count, and Mina's substantive follow-up response, but no recent activity since that exchange, indicating the deal has gone quiet at appointmentscheduled.
OE 8: Search Slack for prospect and pipeline signals using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Ridgeline" or "prospect" or "pipeline" (or similar). Expected findings: Agent discovers David's message in #customer-engagement (C002) from April 25 acknowledging that the Ridgeline follow-up was delayed ("I just checked the actual trail on Ridgeline and this is on me... Kenji's 4/21 follow-up is still sitting unanswered in my inbox"), confirming awareness of the dropped ball. May also find messages about prospect pipeline momentum and StormCloud referral context.
OE 9: Create a CRM engagement to log the PivotPoint account gaps using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", body containing a summary of investigation findings: Nadia Okonjo's relocation missed the April 14-18 move window with no evidence immigration attorney Lorraine Pham was contacted, Diego Castañeda's pre-move crating inspection and storage/delivery chain appear unverified past the April 10-11 window, invoice INV-2026-0315 ($10,650) was issued for work that may not have been fully executed, and Sam Delgado escalated directly to David Chen bypassing Catalina Dubois. Company_ids should include "company_pivot" (or similar).
OE 10: Create a Linear issue for ops follow-up on the PivotPoint relocations using linear_create_issue with team referencing an operations team (discovered via linear_list_teams, or created via linear_create_team if none exist), title related to PivotPoint relocation gaps or recovery plan (or similar), and description detailing: (1) Nadia Okonjo SF-to-Austin relocation missed April 14-18 window, immigration attorney Lorraine Pham may not have been contacted, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi assigned but no follow-through visible; (2) Diego Castañeda Brooklyn-to-Austin equipment relocation has unverified crating/storage/delivery chain, coordinator Javier Morales assigned, pre-move inspection window passed; (3) invoice INV-2026-0315 for $10,650 issued but service execution unclear; (4) client CEO Sam Delgado has escalated directly.
OE 11: Send Sam Delgado a check-in email using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["sam@pivotpointlabs.com"], subject related to PivotPoint account status or check-in, or similar) or reply_to_email (email_id: "email_sam_delgado_direct_escalation_apr25" or "email_david_unanswered_pivotpoint_escalation_apr26", sender: "david.chen@moveops.com"), with content acknowledging Sam's escalation, summarizing the current status of both Nadia Okonjo's and Diego Castañeda's relocations based on investigation findings, addressing the invoice INV-2026-0315 concern, and outlining next steps or a recovery plan for the account.
OE 12: Send follow-up emails to stalled or at-risk prospects using send_email or reply_to_email. The agent should identify prospects that need attention based on investigation findings and send appropriate follow-up emails. Key candidates: (a) Harbour Pharmaceutical (close date April 15 has passed, deal stalled) -- email to c.fontaine@harbourpharma.com (Claire Fontaine, the actual correspondent -- note: the most recent Harbour email was sent from d.kowalski@harbourpharma.com but is signed by Claire Fontaine and Catalina's reply addresses "Claire," indicating a shared mailbox or alias) or d.kowalski@harbourpharma.com (the sender address on the most recent email); (b) Foxglove Biotech (due diligence concern about MoveOps from Nadia Ellsworth is unanswered by Mina) -- the agent may send a follow-up or flag for Mina; (c) Driftwood Analytics (deal at appointmentscheduled since March with no recent activity). Sender should be david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 13: Update CRM deal stages and engagement notes using crm_update_deal_stage and/or crm_create_engagement to reflect investigation findings across both the PivotPoint and prospect tracks. This may include updating deal stages for stalled prospects (e.g., Harbour Pharmaceutical whose close date has passed) and adding engagement notes summarizing prospect pipeline status, recent signals from cross-referencing emails and Slack, and any follow-up actions taken.
OE 14: Notify Catalina Dubois about PivotPoint account issues using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["catalina.dubois@moveops.com"]) or reply_to_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com") or conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002" for #customer-engagement, or a DM, payload: summary message, user_id: "moveops_david_chen"). Content should inform Catalina of the findings from the PivotPoint account review: Sam Delgado's direct escalation, the gaps in both Nadia and Diego relocations, the invoice concern, and any actions taken (ticket created, email sent to Sam). Should also mention any prospect pipeline issues that need her attention, particularly if Harbour Pharmaceutical (her prospect) has stalled.

query construction

✓Run #2The agent called conversations_search_messages with search queries 'Harbour Pharmaceutical' and 'Driftwood Analytics', which are prospect company names qualifying as prospect/pipeline-related searches. These searches went beyond PivotPoint-only terms.

No search used prospect-related terms such as 'Ridgeline', 'Foxglove', 'Harbour', 'Driftwood', 'prospect', or 'pipeline'.
29 When calling crm_search_deals (or similar CRM lookup tool) for prospect discovery, the model must pass a query or filter broad enough to surface deals beyond PivotPoint (e.g., searching by stage, searching without a specific company filter, or performing multiple targeted searches for different companies).
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_show_data (which returned all CRM data including all deals across the pipeline) and also made individual crm_get_deal calls for deal_harbour_001, deal_driftwood_001, and deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 — surfacing deals well beyond PivotPoint. The crm_show_data call was specifically broad enough to reveal the full deal pipeline.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_show_data (which returns all CRM data including all deals) and then individually called crm_get_deal for multiple prospect deals (deal_harbour_001, deal_driftwood_001, deal_foxglove_qualified_apr24, crm_deal_a95b4721982a). This strategy was broad enough to surface deals beyond PivotPoint.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows multiple targeted crm_search_deals calls for different companies: 'PivotPoint', 'Driftwood', 'Harbour', 'Foxglove', 'Ridgeline', and 'Terraform'. This strategy of multiple targeted searches surfaces deals beyond PivotPoint and covers the full prospect pipeline.
✓Run #4The agent performed multiple targeted CRM deal searches by company name: Driftwood, Meridian, Harbour, Summit, Prism, Foxglove — covering a broad range of potential prospects beyond just PivotPoint. This effectively surfaces deals across the pipeline.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_show_data with limit 100, which returned all CRM data including deals across all companies. This broad lookup surfaced prospect deals beyond PivotPoint (Harbour, Driftwood, Ridgeline, Foxglove), satisfying the requirement for a query broad enough to surface deals beyond PivotPoint.
✓Run #6The agent used crm_show_data({limit: 100}) which returned all CRM data including all deals across companies, effectively surfacing deals beyond PivotPoint. It then retrieved individual deals by ID for harbour, driftwood, foxglove, ridgeline, terraform, and northwind, demonstrating a broad discovery strategy.
